sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: SV Zulte Waregem are in decent form, remaining unbeaten in their last 4 matches with 3 wins and 1 draw. KAA Gent have also shown strength, securing 4 victories in their last 5 games, including a high-scoring 4-2 win over Cercle Brugge. This suggests both teams are confident heading into the match, with Gent slightly edging in recent attacking output.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two head-to-head encounters, KAA Gent have dominated SV Zulte Waregem, winning both matches with high-scoring results (4-2 and 6-2). Historical data indicates Gent's superiority, with 6 consecutive wins against Waregem, often scoring early and capitalizing on defensive lapses.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: KAA Gent, positioned 6th with 17 points, have scored 17 goals this season, showing stronger attacking intent compared to Waregem's 13 goals (8th place, 14 points). Waregem's defense has conceded 13 goals, matching Gent's, but their lower possession stats (47.2%) and fewer big chances created (15 vs. Gent's implied stronger numbers) suggest less control in matches.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Waregem, Jeppe Erenbjerg (4 goals, 1 assist) is a key attacking threat, while Anton Tanghe has been solid defensively with a 7.8 rating in recent games. KAA Gent's Atsuki Ito (3 goals, 1 assist) and goalkeeper Davy Roef (7.62 rating) could be decisive in maintaining their edge.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Gent's historical dominance, recent form, and stronger attacking stats, they are favored to win with a 46% probability per Sofascore. Waregem's home advantage and unbeaten streak provide some hope, but their struggle to keep clean sheets against Gent (6 consecutive games) might prove costly. Expect a competitive match with goals likely on both sides.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, SV Zulte Waregem leads KAA Gent with a commanding 3-0 scoreline at the Elindus Arena in Waregem. The home side has been clinical in front of goal, converting their opportunities with precision through goals from Thomas Claes, Jeppe Erenbjerg, and Benoit Nyssen. Despite KAA Gent controlling more of the ball possession, their inability to create big chances has left them trailing significantly. Zulte Waregem has shown defensive solidity and effectiveness in the final third, which has been the key difference in this first half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw distinct shifts in attacking momentum between the two teams. Initially, KAA Gent dominated the early minutes with sustained pressure in the attacking third, pushing forward with intent. However, their momentum waned as Zulte Waregem took control around the time of their first goal, launching a series of threatening attacking spells. The home side continued to build dangerous moves leading up to their second and third goals, showcasing their ability to capitalize on key moments. Towards the latter part of the half, Gent attempted to regain some initiative with sporadic forward pushes, but Zulte Waregem managed to maintain their attacking threat until the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: For SV Zulte Waregem, Thomas Claes and Jeppe Erenbjerg have been standout performers, each finding the back of the net and posing constant threats. Benoit Nyssen also made his mark with a well-taken goal, contributing to the team's dominant scoreline. Joseph Opoku has been instrumental in midfield, providing a crucial assist and creating a big chance. On the other side, KAA Gent's Franck Surdez showed some promise with a couple of efforts on target, but overall, their attack lacked the sharpness needed to break through Zulte Waregem's defense. Mathias Delorge has tried to spark something in midfield for Gent, though with limited success so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: SV Zulte Waregem has executed a near-perfect game plan in the first half, balancing defensive organization with lethal counter-attacks. Their ability to win duels, especially in the air, has helped them maintain control during key moments. KAA Gent, despite holding more possession and entering the final third more frequently, has struggled to turn their territorial advantage into meaningful chances. Their defense has been exposed on multiple occasions, particularly during quick transitions by Zulte Waregem. Gent will need to tighten up at the back and find a way to penetrate the home side's defensive structure if they hope to mount a comeback.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Heading into the second half, SV Zulte Waregem will likely aim to protect their lead while seeking opportunities to exploit Gent's defensive vulnerabilities on the counter. KAA Gent, on the other hand, faces an uphill battle and will need to adopt a more aggressive approach to claw back into the game. They might push more players forward, which could open up spaces for Zulte Waregem to strike again. Given the current dynamics, it seems probable that the home side will look to manage the game, but Gent's desperation could lead to an open, high-scoring second half if they find their rhythm.
